SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.datasource

Class ListingTable

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.datasource.ListingTable
public class ListingTable extends Object
A file-backed listing table.

Created from a ListingTableConfig that specifies the paths, options, and schema. A listing table scans files in one or more directories, parsing them using a FileFormat.

Example: 

ListingTableUrl url = ListingTableUrl.parse("/path/to/data/");
ListingOptions options = ListingOptions.builder(myFormat)
    .collectStat(true)
    .build();
ListingTableConfig config = new ListingTableConfig(url)
    .withListingOptions(options)
    .withSchema(mySchema);
ListingTable table = new ListingTable(config);
ctx.registerListingTable("my_table", table, allocator);
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: ListingTable

  • Constructor Details

    • ListingTable

      public ListingTable(ListingTableConfig config)
      Creates a listing table from the given config.

      The config must have listing options and schema set.

      Parameters:
      config - the listing table configuration
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if options or schema are not set on the config
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTable::try_new

  • Method Details

    • tablePaths

      public List<ListingTableUrl> tablePaths()
      Returns the table paths. Mirrors Rust's ListingTable.table_paths.

      Example: 

      ListingTable table = new ListingTable(config);
List<ListingTableUrl> paths = table.tablePaths();
String firstPath = paths.get(0).getUrl();
System.out.println("First table path: " + firstPath);
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTable::table_paths

    • options

      public ListingOptions options()
      Returns the listing options.

      Example: 

      ListingTable table = new ListingTable(config);
ListingOptions opts = table.options();
FileFormat format = opts.format();
String extension = opts.fileExtension();
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTable::options

    • schema

      public org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema()
      Returns the table schema.

      Example: 

      ListingTable table = new ListingTable(config);
Schema schema = table.schema();
List<Field> fields = schema.getFields();
System.out.println("Table has " + fields.size() + " columns");
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTable::schema
